# Feature: MVE Multi-Textblock Order + Scene Sync Refresh

<!-- auto-generated by /implement on 2026-07-06 — fixes audio dialog stacking bugs -->

## Intent

In audio (Hörspiel) projects, multiple MVE text blocks in one scene must stack in stable order without overlapping. Creating a second block must assign a unique `orderIndex`, and content-driven scene resize must refresh the Structure timeline UI (pink scene shell grows/shrinks).

Roadmap: MVE Slice 1 (Stufe B) — `mve-line-duration-width.md`

## Preconditions

- Local desktop (`VITE_SCRIPTONY_RUNTIME=local`), audio project open
- Dialog lane with character assigned, scene visible on structure timeline
- MVE enabled (`useMveLines`)

## Happy Path

- [x] Second text block in same scene gets `orderIndex` greater than the first (not scene list index) — _unit: `nextLineOrderIndexForScene`_
- [x] After creating second empty block, scene shell grows to include default shell duration (contentDriven) — _logic + harness `scene-grown-shell`; Tauri E2E pending_
- [x] After typing text in first block (with second present), blocks stack sequentially without horizontal overlap — _Playwright QA harness_
- [ ] Pink scene track width updates after MVE scene resize (timeline reload, not stale `timelineData`) — _code wired; Tauri manual pending_

## Edge Cases

- [x] Two blocks same scene, same character: sequential placement left-to-right — _Playwright_
- [ ] Scene sync failure: MVE lines still save; no silent crash — _not exercised_

## Regression

- [x] First sole empty text block still spans full scene width — _Playwright `stack-two-correct-order`_
- [x] WPM width after save unchanged — _existing unit tests_
- [ ] Film project: no contentDriven resize (policy unchanged) — _unit policy only_

## Assumptions

- `reloadTimelineDataFromSource` is the canonical editor refresh after structure pct patches
- `orderIndex` scope: per scene + character (text-only lines on same dialog lane)

## Screenshots

| Step | Filename                         |
| ---- | -------------------------------- |
| 1    | `01-two-blocks-stacked.png`      |
| 2    | `02-scene-grew-after-second.png` |

## Implementation Notes

- `nextLineOrderIndexForScene()` — per scene + character, excludes audio-bound lines
- `handleAddMveTextBlock` uses it instead of `scenes.findIndex` (bug fix)
- `useMveLines.onStructureSynced` → `reloadTimelineDataFromSource` in editor after content-driven resize
- `resolveMveLineVisualSpanMap()` — cumulative min-width stacking (fixes WPM vs 220px overlap)
- QA: `/#qa-mve-textblock-order-sync`, `.qa/runs/2026-07-06-mve-textblock-order-sync.spec.ts`
- Tests: `resolve-scene-at-timeline-sec.test.ts`, `mve-dialog-clip-layout.test.ts`
- Known limitation: block width still updates after debounced save (800ms), not live while typing
- Tauri manual: scene shell refresh after Plus + text save still needs smoke on real project
